While selling intensity has eased, a turnaround in flows is unlikely without significant macroeconomic improvement.

Families struggled to cook and preserve meat amid sweltering heat, leading to widespread protests. Despite official assurances, essential services failed, forcing many to resort to costly alternatives and endure immense hardship during the festival.

title: Inside Track: Rulers Rule Club Class
Are human soldiers being replaced? How Ukraine's 'silent death' robots are fighting Russia
description: Ukraine's war against Russia is transforming into a conflict dominated by machines. Robots and drones are now performing dangerous missions, replacing human soldiers on the frontlines. This shift is driven by Ukraine's troop shortages. Unmanned systems are carrying explosives, delivering supplies, and providing reconnaissance. This technological revolution offers a glimpse into the future of combat.

title: Ghaziabad 'Bakrid invite' murder: Main accused Asad shot dead in encounter
description: Ghaziabad police have shot dead Asad, the main accused in the fatal stabbing of Class 11 student Surya Chauhan. Asad, who was on the run with a Rs 50,000 bounty, allegedly opened fire on police during an attempt to apprehend him. He succumbed to injuries at the hospital. An injured constable is also undergoing treatment.

title: More muscle, less fat, same deadly swing: How Bhuvneshwar is defying age at 36
description: At 36, Bhuvneshwar Kumar is still outrunning younger pacers. While generations of fast bowlers have come and gone since his IPL debut in 2011, the RCB veteran remains a constant. TOI traces the fitness transformation, meticulous preparation and daily discipline that have helped him stay relevant in cricket's most demanding format for more than a decade.

Shivakumar's assets include substantial immovable and movable properties, with liabilities of Rs 265 crore.

This success positions it for further box office milestones.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chiefs eye Bears TE and Giants pass rusher
description: The Kansas City Chiefs have been linked to two potential trade targets in ESPN's offseason simulation. One proposal would bring Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet to Kansas City for a 2027 third-round pick, while another would send New York Giants edge rusher Kayvon Thibodeaux to the Chiefs for a 2027 fourth-rounder. The rumors address Kansas City's needs at tight end depth behind Travis Kelce and pass-rush help entering the 2026 season.
